issues.location: Vision globale du portefeuille — feature section image
issues.description: The portfolio screenshot uses an English-language UI image (alt='Portfolio', filename contains 'en.png'). All other feature images are French UI screenshots. This creates a language inconsistency visible to French-speaking visitors.

All FAQ accordion toggle icons render as empty spans (Font Awesome Duotone icons with no character content). The icon glyphs are missing, leaving a blank space where the expand/collapse indicator should appear.

All feature sections (Agent IA, Email Bilan de santé, Flash Report, etc.) use H3 headings directly under the H1, with no H2 grouping them. There is no section title introducing this feature list, so the heading hierarchy jumps from H1 to H3, which is both semantically incorrect and visually jarring — a visitor skimming headings gets no context for what these features relate to.

issues.location: Vision globale du portefeuille — feature body copy
issues.description: The body copy echoes the H1 almost word-for-word ('80 projets côté DSI. 80 côté Marketing. 80 côté Finance.'). While intentional for emphasis, repeating the exact H1 phrasing verbatim in a mid-page feature section feels like a copy-paste stub rather than a developed explanation of the feature.

The nav secondary CTA 'Demander une démo' links to '/fr/meetings-pages', which is the same destination as the hero primary CTA 'Réservez une démo'. On a landing page, this is expected, but the label inconsistency ('Demander' vs 'Réservez') between nav and hero for identical destinations may confuse users.

issues.location: Trusted logos section — 'Ils nous font confiance'
issues.description: All five logo images have empty alt attributes. While decorative logos can have empty alt text, at least one brand name (e.g., SNCF, Kiabi) is recognisable and meaningful to French visitors — missing alt text harms accessibility and SEO for a trust-building section.

Each FAQ accordion button contains a Font Awesome icon <span> that renders as a blank/invisible character because Font Awesome 6 Duotone is not loaded (the span content is empty in the HTML). Every question row shows an empty square instead of an icon, making the UI look broken.

issues.location: Feature sections — 'Agent IA Brief projet' and 'Agent IA Découpage projet'
issues.description: Two consecutive feature sections are branded as AI agent features ('Agent IA Brief projet', 'Agent IA Découpage projet') while the rest of the page is specifically about capacity planning. These sections feel misplaced on a capacity-planning landing page and appear to be copy-pasted from a different product page without contextual adaptation.

issues.location: Feature sections — all H3 headings (8 sections)
issues.description: There is no H2 grouping or section title introducing the feature alternating-layout sections. The page jumps directly from the trust-logo band into a series of H3-headed feature sections with no parent H2, breaking the logical heading hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3) and leaving the feature block without a label for users or crawlers.

The nav 'Demander une démo' button links to /fr/meetings-pages, and the hero primary CTA 'Réservez une démo' also links to /fr/meetings-pages. Both are correct individually, but the secondary hero CTA '📘 Découvrir le guide Capacity Planning' links to /fr/livre-blanc/capacity-planning — this destination should be verified to exist; if the livre-blanc page is not yet live, clicking it will produce a 404.

The subtitle uses colloquial language ('pas au feeling') which may suit the brand tone, but immediately follows a very formal H1. The tonal shift is abrupt enough that a human reviewer would flag it for copy consistency review.

Eight consecutive alternating-layout sections with no visual break (no CTA, no divider, no section header between them) creates an extremely long scroll of identical-pattern blocks. A mid-funnel CTA or a grouping header is missing, likely causing drop-off before users reach the FAQ.

issues.location: FAQ — 'Est-ce que ça suffit pour un vrai capacitaire ?' (truncated in HTML)
issues.description: The HTML is truncated at this FAQ item's answer. If the answer content is empty or cut off in the final rendered output, this is a stub section visible to users. Needs verification that the full FAQ answer is present.

All feature sections use H3 headings directly after the H2 'Ils nous font confiance' trust bar, but there is no intermediate H2 grouping these feature sections together. The heading hierarchy jumps from page H1 → H2 (trust bar) → H3 (features) with no section-level H2 framing the feature showcase, making the document outline semantically broken and hard to navigate.

Each accordion button contains an empty <span> styled with Font Awesome Duotone, but no icon character or Unicode code point is provided inside it, so the icon slot renders as a blank ~2.3rem-wide invisible box. Users see a gap/empty space rather than the expand/collapse icon.

The secondary CTA button uses a raw emoji '▶️' inline in the button label text. On some OS/browser combinations this renders as a colour emoji that clashes with the orange-bright button background, and the combined string '▶️ Découvrir l'outil PPM en vidéo (5 min)' is unusually long for a primary-area hero button and likely wraps or overflows on mid-range viewports.

issues.location: Hero section — missing product screenshot / hero visual
issues.description: The hero section has no product image or visual below the CTAs before the trust bar. Every feature section has an image, but the hero itself is text-only with a gradient background. For a PPM landing page this leaves the top half of the page visually empty and reduces immediate credibility — likely a missing component compared to the live page which shows a dashboard screenshot.

The pricing answer reads 'Prix accessible. Tarification sur mesure selon la taille de votre organisation. Parlons-en lors d'une démo.' — the first sentence 'Prix accessible.' is a sentence fragment that sounds abrupt and unfinished, providing no actual pricing signal. It may have been truncated from a fuller sentence.

The top-right nav CTA 'Demander une démo' links to '/fr/meetings-pages', which is the same destination as the hero primary CTA 'Réservez une démo'. This is intentional duplication, but on a dedicated LP the nav demo button destination should be consistent with the LP-specific demo flow — worth confirming this is not accidentally pointing to the generic meeting page instead of a PPM-specific one.

issues.location: Trust bar — 'Ils nous font confiance' section
issues.description: The section heading 'Ils nous font confiance' uses a gradient text style but there are only 5 logos and no customer count or supporting sub-copy (e.g. '+200 entreprises'). The section feels thin compared to a typical trust bar and may look like a stub to reviewers expecting more social proof context.

The answer 'Prix accessible. Parlons-en lors d'une démo.' is a non-answer that gives the user zero useful information. It reads as a placeholder that was never filled in, which erodes trust on a sales-focused landing page.

issues.location: Section 'AirSaas : la couche business au-dessus de Jira'
issues.description: This section renders as a standalone heading + one-line paragraph with no visual content, card, or image — it functions as an orphaned intro with no body. It looks like an incomplete or stub section rather than a finished page block.

issues.location: Feature section — 'Les objectifs PI ne disparaissent plus'
issues.description: The image used (Capacity screen.webp) depicts a capacity planning screen, but the section heading and copy are about PI objective tracking. The image does not match the section's stated purpose, which is confusing and misleading.

The icon spans inside each accordion button use Font Awesome 6 Duotone via a font-family style, but the character code is empty — no glyph is rendered. Every accordion question appears without its icon, leaving a visible blank space to the left of each question label.

The subtitle paragraph contains a nested

tag inside a inside another

('On ne remplace pas Jira...' section). This is invalid HTML and causes the inner paragraph to break out of the outer one in browsers, producing unexpected spacing and rendering artifacts.

The nav CTA 'Demander une démo' links to /fr/meetings-pages, which is a generic meetings path rather than a PI Planning–specific demo or at minimum a tagged URL. The hero CTA 'Réservez une démo' links to the same untagged URL, missing an opportunity for LP-specific attribution.

The H1 'Miro + Jira + PowerBI : ce n'est pas comme ça qu'un RTE embarque les métiers.' names two direct competitors (Miro, PowerBI) prominently in the page title. While intentional as a comparison hook, this is a brand risk on a page that is indexed and crawled — worth a deliberate sign-off.

issues.location: Feature section — 'Capacity' image alt text (second alt='Capacity')
issues.description: Two distinct sections both use alt='Capacity' for different screenshots (one for capacity planning, one for PI objective tracking). The second usage is factually incorrect as an alt description for a PI-objective-tracking visual, which hurts accessibility.

issues.location: "Vous n'entendrez bientôt plus ces phrases ..." section
issues.description: The section title promises a list of recurring complaints/phrases users won't hear anymore, but the body contains zero such phrases — instead it repeats the sponsor onboarding description. The entire pain-point content block is missing.

issues.location: Feature sections ("Vous n'entendrez bientôt plus ces phrases" and split section)
issues.description: The heading 'Ajoutez les sponsors sur vos projets' and its body copy appear verbatim twice back-to-back — once in the centered section and once in the left/right split section. This is clear content duplication suggesting the rest of the feature list was never rendered.

issues.location: "Vous n'entendrez bientôt plus ces phrases" section
issues.description: The section contains only one feature sub-item ('Ajoutez les sponsors sur vos projets') where the live page shows multiple distinct features. The page is severely truncated and looks unfinished to any visitor.

issues.location: "Vous n'entendrez bientôt plus ces phrases" section — paragraph
issues.description:
A

element is nested directly inside another

element (invalid HTML: `

...

Ajoutez les sponsors...

`), which browsers will silently break into unexpected DOM structure, potentially causing visible layout splits.

There is no testimonial, social proof, or CTA section before the footer. The page jumps from two near-identical feature blocks directly to the footer, leaving the bottom of the page feeling abruptly cut off.

issues.location: H3 inside "Vous n'entendrez bientôt plus ces phrases" section
issues.description: The H3 'Ajoutez les sponsors sur vos projets' is a functional instruction, not a pain-point phrase — it does not match the section's framing of 'phrases you'll no longer hear', creating a semantic mismatch that would confuse readers.

Both the hero CTA ('Je veux une démo') and the nav CTA ('Demander une démo') link to /fr/meetings-pages, which is consistent, but the hero label 'Je veux une démo' is slightly informal/first-person in a way that's inconsistent with the more standard 'Demander une démo' used elsewhere on the same page.

issues.location: Feature section — 'Chaque équipe définie ses prios'
issues.description: The H3 heading contains a grammatical error: 'définie' should be 'définit' (third-person singular present indicative of 'définir'). This is a user-visible French grammar mistake.

issues.location: Third feature section — 'Organisez la roadmap de façon éclairée'
issues.description: The body copy contains a typo: 'porfolios' should be 'portfolios'. Visible spelling mistake in product copy.

All three FAQ accordion buttons have an empty <span> intended to render a Font Awesome Duotone icon, but the span contains no icon character/code. The icon area will render as a blank space, making the FAQ buttons look visually broken.

issues.location: "La manière la plus simple d'y voir clair" section — body paragraph
issues.description: The paragraph body uses an invalid emoji shortcode ':chacun' — the text reads '…Impossible de mettre 5 projets en Top 1 :chacun a une priorité…' which looks like a Webflow/CMS emoji shortcode (':each:' or similar) that was not rendered and is now concatenated into the sentence, making it unreadable.
The copyright year reads '© 2026 AirSaas', which is one year in the future relative to the current period. This looks like a copy error and may confuse visitors.

issues.location: FAQ — 'Si on repriorise en cours de route' answer
issues.description: The answer ends abruptly without a period: '…en expliquant à tout le monde les raisons' — the sentence appears truncated and lacks a closing punctuation mark, suggesting the copy may be cut off.

issues.location: Section 'Mise en place rapide, simple à maintenir dans le temps'
issues.description: The section body is visibly truncated: it reads 'nous avons pris le parti suivant :' and then immediately 'Voici comment nous le concrétisons.' with no actual content between the two sentences. The key value proposition (the parti pris) is missing entirely.

The paragraph ends with 'pour prendre les décisions :' followed by two non-breaking-space placeholder paragraphs (‍‍) and nothing else. The actual list of decisions is absent — the section is effectively empty.

issues.location: Section 'Les Scénarios' > subsection 'Trouvez le scénario qui fonctionne'
issues.description: The rich-text body ends with two consecutive zero-width non-joiner placeholder paragraphs ('‍' twice), which will render as blank lines and look broken to a visitor. No actual supporting content follows the single introductory sentence.

This subsection (an H3 with body copy) is placed in its own full-padding <section> element, completely disconnected from the parent 'Les Scénarios' H2 section. It appears as an orphaned section with no H2 ancestor, breaking the logical heading hierarchy for users and screen readers.

The hero screenshot has alt='Capacity Marketing', which is a generic internal label rather than a meaningful description of the capacity-planning view being shown. This is both an accessibility issue and a content quality issue.

The section contains only one feature subsection ('Trouvez le scénario qui fonctionne') with a single short paragraph. Given the heading implies multiple scenarios/use cases, the section feels sparse and likely has missing feature cards or additional subsections from the source.

issues.location: Navigation CTA — 'Demander une démo' / Hero CTA 'Je veux une démo'
issues.description: Both CTAs link to '/fr/meetings-pages', which is an unusual slug (likely a Webflow artifact). If this page does not exist in the rebuild, users clicking the primary CTAs will hit a 404, blocking the main conversion goal.

The Font Awesome icon spans inside each FAQ accordion button render as empty boxes (no visible icon character in the HTML output). The span content is blank, so users see an empty space where a visual indicator (e.g., chevron or plus) should appear.

issues.location: Feature sections — all H3 headings under H1
issues.description: The page jumps directly from H1 (hero) to a H2 (intro section) and then to H3 headings for all feature detail sections, with no H2 grouping those feature sections. This creates an illogical heading hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3 with no intermediate H2 for the feature group).

issues.location: Section 'Prenez en compte le coût humain des projets'
issues.description: The body copy contains a typo: 'Suivre les budgets project' should be 'projets' (pluriel). Also 'fraicheur' is missing the accent (should be 'fraîcheur') — these are visible copy errors, not source faithfulness issues.

issues.location: Hero CTA button / Nav CTA button
issues.description: Both the hero CTA 'Je veux une démo' and the nav 'Demander une démo' link to '/fr/meetings-pages', which appears to be an internal page slug rather than a demo booking flow. If this slug resolves to a 404 or wrong page in the rebuild, it is a blocking functional issue on every CTA on the page.

issues.location: Section intro — 'Un suivi facile sans prise de tête'
issues.description:
The paragraph content is wrapped in a redundant nested structure: a

element is rendered inside a inside another

, producing invalid HTML (block element inside inline element). This could cause rendering inconsistencies across browsers.

issues.location: Feature section image alt text — 'ADD EXPENSES' image
issues.description: The alt text 'ADD EXPENSES' is a raw CMS/asset filename artifact rather than a descriptive French alt text appropriate for the audience and context of this French product page.

issues.location: Feature section image alt text — 'Analytical axes' image
issues.description: The alt text 'Analytical axes' is in English on a French-language page, appearing to be an untranslated CMS filename. It should be 'Axes analytiques' or a descriptive French equivalent.

The second FAQ item has aria-expanded='false' and grid-template-rows:'0fr' with opacity:0, meaning its answer is hidden on load. While this is expected accordion behavior, there is no visible expand/collapse indicator (icon is blank — see P1 issue), so users have no affordance to know the item is interactive.
The answer contains a grammar error: "avec une niveau de sécurité au top" — 'une' should be 'un' ("un niveau"). This is a user-visible typo in a prominent FAQ.

issues.location: Section 'Prenons de la hauteur' — bullet list item 1
issues.description: The copy reads "Pas le bon niveau de d'abstraction" — there is a double apostrophe/article collision ('de d'abstraction'). Should be 'niveau d'abstraction'.

issues.location: Page — no image in hero section
issues.description: The hero section contains only a heading, paragraph and CTA button but no product screenshot or visual. All three feature sections below include images. A missing hero visual is an obvious layout gap compared to standard product pages and the live site.

issues.location: Section 'Prenons de la hauteur' — body paragraph
issues.description:
The section body renders a

tag nested inside another

tag (a wrapping raw

and

    elements inside a

    ). This is invalid HTML that will cause rendering issues in most browsers — the inner

    tags will be hoisted out, breaking the intended layout.
Copyright reads '© 2026 AirSaas' — the year 2026 is a future date and looks like a placeholder or typo; should be the current or launch year (e.g. 2024 or 2025).

issues.location: Hero CTA and Nav — 'Demander une démo' / 'Je veux une démo'
issues.description: Both CTA buttons link to '/fr/meetings-pages'. If this is a demo booking page that should be '/fr/meetings-page' (singular) or another route, this could be a dead link. The path should be verified against the live routing.

issues.location: Section 'Plus besoin d'aller à la pêche aux informations'
issues.description: This section has a heading and one short paragraph but no accompanying image or additional visual, unlike all three feature sections that follow it. It looks visually sparse and inconsistent — likely missing a screenshot or illustration from the source.

issues.location: Section 2 h2 / 'Le rapport flash désormais enmultilingue'
issues.description: The H2 heading reads 'enmultilingue' (no space) — a word-break bug that renders as a single unrecognised word on screen. This is distinct from the Webflow source quirk allowance because it appears in a rendered heading, not inside a <strong> tag.

issues.location: Section with H3 'Animer une réunion…' (appears twice)
issues.description: The H3 heading 'Animer une réunion, aligner les parties prenantes, on parle le même language !' and its paragraph body are duplicated verbatim: once as a standalone section (the fourth feature section) and again as a standalone 'callout' section just before the footer. Users see the same block repeated.

issues.location: Section 2 body paragraph / 'Le rapport flash désormais…'
issues.description:
A
    list is nested directly inside a

    tag, which is invalid HTML and will cause the paragraph to collapse mid-render in most browsers, potentially hiding the bullet list entirely.

After the H1 in the hero, Section 2 and Section 3 both use H2 but then Sections 4–6 jump to H3 with no H2 parent — the heading hierarchy is broken (H1 → H2 → H2 → H3 without a containing H2 group), which hurts SEO and screen-reader navigation.

issues.location: H3 'Animer une réunion… on parle le même language'
issues.description: 'language' is the English spelling; the correct French word is 'langage'. This typo appears in both duplicate instances of the heading.

issues.location: Section 3 body / 'Vos chefs de projets et PO vont adorer'
issues.description: The section ends with the stub sentence 'Voici comment nous le concrétisons.' but there is no follow-up content, image, or feature list in this section — it reads as an incomplete or truncated section whose body was never filled in.
Missing space between two sentences: 'Votre maison mère en Allemagne ?Marre de passer…' — the question mark and the next sentence run together without a space, which reads awkwardly.

issues.location: Nav CTA 'Demander une démo' / Hero CTA 'Je veux une démo'
issues.description: Both CTAs point to '/fr/meetings-pages' which is a generic meetings page, not a product-specific demo request — acceptable globally but on a deep product feature page a more targeted destination (or at minimum query-string context) would be expected.

issues.location: Second feature section — 'AirSaas dans le tooling des missions'
issues.description: The paragraph ends with 'grâce mode multi-workspace' — the word 'au' is missing between 'grâce' and 'mode', making the sentence grammatically broken and unprofessional ('grâce au mode multi-workspace').

issues.location: Second feature section — 'AirSaas dans le tooling des missions'
issues.description: Brand name is written 'Airsaas' (lowercase 's') mid-sentence instead of the correct 'AirSaas', which is a brand consistency error visible to readers.

issues.location: Hero section — H1 paragraph / body copy
issues.description: The hero paragraph body text runs two sentences together without a space or line break between them: '…votre stratégie.Êtes-vous prêt…' — the period and next sentence are concatenated with no whitespace, making it look like a rendering bug.

The page has an H1 followed directly by H3s for all content sections, with no H2 used in the main content body (the 'Ils parlent de nous' press-logo band uses an H2, but the three feature sections skip H2 entirely). This breaks logical heading hierarchy and harms SEO and accessibility.

issues.location: Page — missing CTA buttons in feature sections
issues.description: None of the three main feature sections ('Se former entre experts', 'AirSaas dans le tooling', 'You never walk alone') contain any CTA button or link, while the live page includes call-to-action links in each block. The 'Partenaires, cette communauté de 200+ experts vous intéresse ?' sentence at the end of the third section clearly implies a CTA that is absent.

issues.location: Third feature section — 'You never walk alone'
issues.description: The image used is the LPDT logotype on a white SVG background, which renders as a logo floating awkwardly in a large tinted container rather than a meaningful illustration — likely a placeholder or missing proper asset for this section.

The H1 'Surdélivrer vos missions avec AirSaas' uses an infinitive verb form as a heading, which is grammatically unusual in French for a page title aimed at consultants/experts; the live site likely uses a more direct formulation. Minor but may read as awkward to native French speakers.

issues.location: Section 'Plus qu'une solution de reporting flash'
issues.description: The paragraph reads 'En du flash report automatisé, les autres fonctionnalités AirSaas…' — the sentence is grammatically broken and truncated mid-thought. The word 'En' is followed by nothing meaningful, making the copy unintelligible to users.

issues.location: Section 'Les bonnes pratiques de flash report' (H2)
issues.description:
This section has only a heading and a single brief intro paragraph but no actual content — no best-practice items, cards, or list items are rendered. It appears to be a stub with body copy that ends with a
and nothing else, leaving an empty section visible on the page.

issues.location: Section 'Plus qu'une solution de reporting flash' (H2)
issues.description: Same structural issue as the best-practices section: the section contains only a heading and a broken one-liner with no feature cards, list, or supporting content rendered below it. Users see an incomplete section.

The H1 text contains a zero-width joiner character (‍) between 'de' and 'flash': 'La solution de‍flash report'. This renders as no space between the two words in some environments, producing 'deflash report' visually.

issues.location: Feature section 'Une vision Kanban qui simplifie votre gouvernance'
issues.description: The section heading promises a Kanban view, but the body copy describes a dashboard for centralising decisions — no Kanban-specific content is mentioned. The screenshot (Portfolio project filter open) also appears unrelated to a Kanban board, creating a mismatch between heading and content.

issues.location: Feature section 'Mettez en valeur le ressenti de vos équipes'
issues.description: The image used (metier-it.svg — an IT/métier illustration) is generic and unrelated to team sentiment or reporting, making the visual feel like a placeholder rather than a purposeful illustration for this feature.

issues.location: Nav CTA 'Demander une démo' / Hero CTA 'Réservez une démo'
issues.description: Both CTAs point to /fr/meetings-pages rather than a dedicated flash-report demo or contact page. While acceptable if intentional, the inconsistent label ('Demander une démo' vs 'Réservez une démo') for the same destination creates a confusing mixed message within the same page.

issues.location: Press logos section 'Ils parlent de nous'
issues.description: The section heading 'Ils parlent de nous' (They talk about us) is used as an H2 with gradient treatment typically reserved for value-proposition headings. On a solution/feature page this heading style is oversized and the press-bar section does not include quotes or article titles, making it look like a stub logo wall without context.

issues.location: Section 'Plus qu'une solution de reporting flash : un outil PPM projet moderne et simplissime'
issues.description: This section contains only a heading and a single introductory paragraph with no feature cards, list, or visual content below it. It renders as a visually empty stub that introduces features never displayed, leaving users with an incomplete section.

issues.location: Section '3 règles d'or pour utiliser votre flash report projet à bon escient'
issues.description: This section announces '3 règles d'or' but contains only a short teaser paragraph and no actual rules, cards, or list items. The promised content is entirely missing, making this a broken stub section.

issues.location: Feature section 'Des intégrations natives pour éviter le report de données manuel'
issues.description: The body copy repeats the same information twice in consecutive paragraphs — both paragraphs list the same integrations (Jira, ClickUp, Asana, Monday, Microsoft Teams, Zendesk) in nearly identical wording, which reads as a copy-paste duplication error.

issues.location: HTML body (truncated) — last visible section 'Vos fiches de cadrage de projet collaboratives'
issues.description: The page HTML is visibly truncated mid-sentence ('Homogénéiser la culture projet de votre entreprise, et faites monter les c'). This means at least one section is cut off and any subsequent sections (testimonials, CTA, footer) may be missing or broken in the render.

issues.location: Feature sections using 'Une structure fixe hyper-lisible' and 'Votre reporting : l'allié pour vous aider dans vos décisions'
issues.description: Both of these text-left sections use 'lg:pl-[10rem] lg:pr-0' layout but their image panels use 'background-color:var(--color-primary-5)' — identical styling to each other — whereas alternating sections are supposed to use 'var(--color-prevention-10)'. Two consecutive left-text sections with the same background color breaks the alternating visual rhythm.

The section heading 'Ils parlent de nous' implies press/media mentions, but no pull quotes, article titles, or links accompany the logos. Without any context, it looks like a generic logo strip with a misleading heading.

The hero section contains an H1, but the two stub sections ('Plus qu'une solution…' and '3 règles d'or…') each use H2 with no H3 or body content children, while all the feature alternating sections below them use H3 directly. The heading hierarchy jumps from H2 (empty sections) to H3 (feature sections) without a coherent structural parent-child relationship.

The nav CTA 'Demander une démo' links to '/fr/meetings-pages', as does the hero CTA 'Réservez une démo'. Both share the same destination, but the nav uses the French label while potentially pointing to an English-slug page, which may cause confusion or a 404 if the route isn't defined.

issues.location: H2 section — 'Enfin l'outil PPM que toutes les Directions attendaient'
issues.description: This H2 section contains only the heading and no body content, image, or CTA — it renders as a visually isolated, empty-looking banner with no supporting text or visual element.

issues.location: Section with standalone paragraph 'Problématique ciblée à la date de création'
issues.description: This short paragraph appears as a standalone section with no heading or image context — it looks like a table caption or label that was meant to accompany a table/chart (likely a comparison table of PPM tools) that is completely missing from the rebuild.

issues.location: Section 'Un changement de paradigme…' — paragraph about Maslow pyramids
issues.description: The text explicitly references 'ces pyramides de Maslow' but no images of the pyramids are rendered — the visual asset is missing, leaving a dangling reference that confuses readers.

Every H3 content section is a separate full-width section with identical large vertical padding (py-[6.25rem] on lg), resulting in enormous whitespace gaps between sections that would look extremely broken on desktop — these sections appear designed as a single scrollable article, not isolated padded blocks.

The H1 reads 'AirSaas : un outil PPM nouvelle génération - Logiciel ppm' which appears to be an SEO title artifact with the suffix '- Logiciel ppm' visible as raw text on-page — this looks unpolished and likely was intended as meta title only, not displayed copy.

issues.location: Section 'Gagnez du temps en reporting' — internal link
issues.description: The link to the flash report points to the absolute live domain URL 'https://www.airsaas.io/fr/solution/flash-report-projet' instead of a relative internal path, breaking environment consistency and potentially routing users away from the rebuild.

issues.location: Section 'Améliorez votre gestion des ressources' — internal link
issues.description: The link 'vision d'ensemble des projets' points to the absolute live URL 'https://www.airsaas.io/fr/gestion-de-projet/capacity-planning' instead of a relative path, same external-domain link issue as above.

issues.location: Section with standalone paragraph about Planview/Sciforma/Ganttic
issues.description: Competitor tool names (Planview, Sciforma, Ganttic) are mentioned by name in body copy with no contextual framing in this section — combined with the missing comparison table, this reads as an orphaned fragment that lacks the visual context (the table) that originally justified naming competitors.

issues.location: Section 'Avancez plus sereinement avec votre équipe'
issues.description: This section contains only an H2 heading with no body content, features, cards, or supporting copy. It renders as a completely empty section visible to users, suggesting the content block failed to render.

issues.location: Section 'Rapide historique des outils de gestion de portefeuille de projets'
issues.description: This H2 section heading has no body content beneath it — the historical context paragraph appears in a separate orphaned section that follows but has no heading association, breaking the narrative and leaving this section visually empty.

issues.location: Long-form body — multiple consecutive bare <section> blocks
issues.description: The page body is fragmented into ~20 individual <section> elements each containing a single paragraph or heading, with no grouping logic. Alternating heading+body sections separated into sibling sections creates orphaned headings and disconnected paragraphs, breaking the reading flow and heading hierarchy (H2 headings have no directly associated content).

issues.location: Section 'L'interface utilisateur comme vecteur…' body copy

issues.location: Section with standalone paragraph — 'AirSaas est donc solution PPM…'
